title: Triphysaria eriantha
intro: Triphysaria eriantha, also known as California jewelflower, is a rare, annual or perennial plant species native to the coastal regions of California and Oregon in the United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Triphysaria eriantha include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Triphysaria eriantha is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Triphysaria eriantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to California, USA. The plant is a member of the genus Triphysaria. Triphysaria eriantha is a perennial herb that is part of the family Scrophulariaceae. It is closely related to other members of the genus, which are also known as fairy foxes.
